title: Today's Tech Brief: A Relaxing Roundup
summary: Cecil Baldwin's tech pet peeves, a probabilistic algorithm for repairing roads, and more

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to Today's Tech Brief. I've got a few interesting stories to share with you today. First up, Cecil Baldwin, the host of Welcome to Night Vale, has some tech pet peeves that I think we can all relate to. From scrolling through movie credits too quickly to auto-playing videos with sound, Cecil is speaking my language here.

According to an article on The Verge, Cecil Baldwin is a busy man, but one thing that really gets under his skin is when movie credits roll too quickly. I mean, who hasn't been there, right? You're trying to read the credits, and it's like they're trying to scroll through a Twitter feed. He also mentioned that he hates when websites automatically play videos with sound. Yeah, that's just a pet peeve of mine too. It's funny how something as simple as a website's settings can make or break our browsing experience.

Next, I came across a research paper that proposes a probabilistic algorithm for repairing all roads in Lebanon via Papal Visits. Now, this might sound a bit far-fetched, but the idea is actually quite interesting. The researchers are using data to optimize the route of Papal Visits to coincide with road repairs. It's a creative solution to a real problem, and I love the out-of-the-box thinking. Who knew the Pope could help fix roads?

My quick read: today's tech stories are less about one giant thunderclap and more about a lively weather pattern. A little platform drama here, a little artificial intelligence irony there, and suddenly the industry has enough material for a very caffeinated morning show.
